title: The quantum group structure of long-range integrable deformations
abstract: Quantum integrable spin chains are known to possess a large family of long-range deformations generated by the local, boost and bilocal operators. Although these deformations are well-understood on the level of the pairwise commuting charges, the underlying quantum group structures had not yet been recognised. In this paper, we provide a quantum group-theoretical description for the family of long-range deformations of arbitrary homogeneous Yang-Baxter integrable spin chains up to first order in the deformation parameter. In particular, we show that the deformations are obtained via a twist of the algebraic structure of the underlying quantum group. This twisting results in a generally non-associative algebra that has a non-trivial Drinfeld associator. The Drinfeld associator is then shown to encode the information about the long-range interaction terms for the integrable spin chain. Moreover, the deformed quantum group is shown to contain a large perturbatively associative substructure, thus ensuring the perturbative integrability of the long-range model. The deformed quantum group provides explicit expressions for the Lax operators and R-matrices of the long-range deformed models, which manifestly satisfy the RLL relation and the Yang-Baxter equation up to first order in the deformation parameter. In order to derive the results, we introduce algebra elements that we call the algebraic charge densities. As a side result, we provide a conjecture for the explicit expressions of the undeformed charge densities in terms of these algebra elements.
summary: This paper provides a new algebraic description of long-range integrable spin chains by showing they arise from a twist of the underlying quantum group. It demonstrates that this twisting leads to a non-associative structure where the Drinfeld associator encodes the long-range interactions. The work provides explicit tools, such as new algebraic charge densities, to study these deformed integrable models.
main_problem: The paper aims to identify the underlying quantum group structure for long-range integrable deformations of homogeneous Yang-Baxter integrable spin chains.
main_result: The authors provide a quantum group-theoretical description showing that these deformations are obtained via a twist of the underlying algebraic structure, resulting in a non-associative algebra characterized by a Drinfeld associator.
method: The study utilizes FRT-bialgebras, the quantum inverse scattering method, and deformation theory, specifically analyzing the twist mechanism and introducing 'algebraic charge densities' via derivatives of the R-matrix.
model_or_system: The research focuses on quantum integrable spin chains, including the Heisenberg (XXX) model, Haldane-Shastry, and Inozemtsev chains, specifically looking at long-range deformations generated by local, boost, and bilocal operators.
key_observables: Conserved charges (local and algebraic), transfer matrix, Lax operators, R-matrices, and the Drinfeld associator.
important_parameters: The deformation parameter (analyzed to first order) and the spectral parameter.
main_assumptions: The results are perturbative and limited to the first order in the deformation parameter; the R-matrix is assumed to be regular and unitary.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of long-range deformations, establishes the FRT-bialgebra framework, applies a twisting mechanism to describe the deformation, proves the existence of the Drinfeld associator, and provides explicit expressions for Lax operators and R-matrices.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
